cliches: Every touchpoint is a conversion event when your funnel is truly omnichannel.
We don't sell software, we sell outcomes.
The funnel is dead. Long live the flywheel.
Land and expand isn't a tactic, it's a religion.
If you're not in the dark funnel, you're already losing the deal.
Net revenue retention is the only metric that matters.
We're not doing demand gen, we're building a demand engine.
Pipeline is a lagging indicator of how well you nurture.
Your aha moment is your acquisition strategy.
Product-led growth means the product is the salesperson.
Category creation is the ultimate growth hack.
We don't chase leads, we listen for signals.
Every customer is a channel when virality is built in.
Retention is the new acquisition.
We turned our customers into our go-to-market motion.
Negative churn is the closest thing to free money in SaaS.
The best CAC is the CAC you never paid because the product sold itself.
Content is the engine; distribution is the flywheel.
Alignment between sales and marketing isn't optional, it's the moat.
We're not optimizing the funnel, we're compounding the loop.
Time to value is the only deadline that matters.
Your ideal customer profile is a living document, not a slide.
Move at the speed of the buyer, or get out of the way.
Growth isn't a team, it's a culture.
When every rep is data-driven, every quarter is predictable.
You don't find product-market fit, you earn it one cohort at a time.
The best demo is the one the customer gives themselves.
Brand is just demand you haven't captured yet.
Stop buying clicks and start earning attention.
Every churned logo is a postmortem you refused to write.
If the funnel leaks, no amount of traffic will save you.
Distribution is the feature; everything else is table stakes.
The deal you don't qualify out is the forecast you can't trust.
Activation is where acquisition goes to prove it was worth it.
Pipeline doesn't lie, but the people building it sometimes do.
Your champion is your sales team when you're not in the room.
Demand you create is a moat; demand you capture is a race.
Always be closing was the old game; always be helping is the new one.
Growth at all costs is how you win the quarter and lose the company.
If it isn't moving the needle, it's just moving your calendar.
The product that sells itself is the only rep that never misses quota.
Pipeline is vanity, win rate is sanity, net revenue retention is reality.
You can't out-spend a leaky funnel, you can only out-fix it.
Every cold email is a small bet that you understand the buyer better than they expect.
The best discovery call ends with the buyer learning something about their own business.
Marketing earns the attention, sales earns the trust, the product earns the renewal.

Expansion is the reward for an onboarding you didn't cut corners on.

The buyer is already two-thirds through the journey before they ever raise their hand.
A category you create is a moat; a category you compete in is a margin war.
Retention is the quietest growth lever and the loudest signal of love.
Discounting is just borrowing revenue from next quarter at a terrible interest rate.
If your CAC is climbing, your story stopped resonating before your spend did.
The flywheel doesn't care how hard you push once, it cares how often you push.
Attribution is a model, not a truth, and the best teams know the difference.
We don't buy attention, we earn it, and earned attention compounds.
Efficient growth isn't slower growth, it's growth that survives the downturn.
The deal you rush to close is the deal you spend two quarters trying to save.
Demand creation is patient; demand capture is greedy; you need both to win.
When the product is the best salesperson, the sales team finally gets to think.
